title: Investigation of (gamma,p)-reactions on zirconium and molybdenium nuclei

x4freetext: Investigation of (gamma,p)-reactions on zirconium
and molybdenium nuclei

x4codes.hlp: Inst. Yadernyh Doslidzhen, N.A.N.Ukraini, Kyiv, Ukraine

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: European Physical Journal A: Hadrons and Nuclei, Vol.58, p.118 (2022)

x4freetext: The linear electron accelerator LUE-40
The molybdenum target had a cylinder form with the
diameter of 7 mm, height of 5.5 mm and weight about
The zirconium target consisted of a rectangular metal
strip 1.5x1.5x0.27 cm in size and weight about 4 g.
HPGe-detectors which have an energy resolution
(FWHM) of 1.2 keV at the 343 keV peak of 175Hf.
Induced gamma-activity was measured.
Experimental data are compared with theoretical
predictions calculated with TALYS-1.9 code.
The yields for the 100Mo(g,n)99Mo and 90Zr(g,n)89Zr
monitor reactions were determined as a result of the
convolution of the tabulated values of the
cross-sections of these reactions for monochromatic
gamma-quanta with a step-width of 1 MeV with the
relative values of the spectrum of bremsstrahlung
gamma-quanta simulated in the Geant4 code.
Uncertainties of half life, irradiation, cooling and
measuring times, A and the absolute content of isotope
in the natural isotopic composition can be ignored.
Error for the net photo-peaks areas.
Error for efficiencies of the detector system.
Error for the absolute emission probabilities
of gamma-rays from decays of the Nb isotopes.
Error for gamma-ray self-absorption.
Error for conversion ratio for the reaction.
Error for the flux-weighted average yield
for the 100Mo(g,n)99Mo monitor reaction.
The total errors were determined by quadrature
of the all presented uncertainties.
